2606.07692.title: BCG-FM: A Foundation Model for Ambient Cardiac Health Sensing
2606.07692.paper_summary: Foundation models for wearable biosignals have matched or exceeded supervised specialists across a range of clinical tasks, yet all rely on modalities that require deliberate user action--wearing a device or visiting a sleep lab. We introduce BCG-FM, the first foundation model for ambient mechanical biosignals. A piezoelectric sensor embedded in the bed surface records ballistocardiography (BCG) each night without user effort; we pretrain BCG-FM with participant-level contrastive learning and using a total of 2.75 million hours of nightly recordings from 145,985 individuals, the largest raw-waveform biosignal pretraining corpus to date. Frozen BCG-FM embeddings achieve 3.26-year MAE on biological-age estimation (the lowest reported for any ambient, contactless modality) and yield clinically relevant discrimination across 15 self-reported health conditions and three independent external cohorts. Pretrained representations from only 500 labeled participants outperform a fully supervised baseline trained on 3,372, and representation quality scales log-linearly with contrastive batch size. These results establish ambient, longitudinal mechanical biosignals as a viable modality for health foundation models.
2606.07692.paper_summary_zh: 可穿戴生物信號的基礎模型在多項臨床任務中已經達到或超越了受監督專家的表現，然而這些模型都依賴於需要用戶主動行動的模式——佩戴設備或訪問睡眠實驗室。我們介紹了BCG-FM，首個用於環境機械生物信號的基礎模型。嵌入床面中的壓電傳感器每晚無需用戶努力即可記錄心搏圖（BCG）；我們使用來自145,985名個體的275萬小時夜間錄音進行參與者級對比學習來預訓練BCG-FM，這是迄今為止最大的原始波形生物信號預訓練語料庫。凍結的BCG-FM嵌入在生物年齡估計上達到3.26年的平均絕對誤差（MAE）（這是任何環境、無接觸模式中報告的最低值），並在15種自我報告的健康狀況和三個獨立的外部隊列中產生臨床相關的區分。僅使用500名標記參與者的預訓練表示超越了在3,372名參與者上訓練的完全監督基線，且表示質量隨對比批次大小呈對數線性增長。這些結果確立了環境、縱向機械生物信號作為健康基礎模型的一種可行模式。
2606.07692.author: Magnus Ruud Kjaer et.al.
2606.07692.authors: Magnus Ruud Kjaer, Haejun Han, Ashish Neupane, David Q. Sun
